nodes.label: Indian PQC Scanner Market Opportunity
nodes.rationale: Zero domestic competition in India for PQC scanners; first-mover opportunity with INR 1-3Cr/yr pricing for large banks; window 2025-2028 before global platforms add Indian coverage

nodes.label: Harvest Now Decrypt Later (HNDL) Threat
nodes.rationale: Adversaries stealing encrypted data today for future quantum decryption; drives urgency for PQC scanning adoption

nodes.label: Mid-Market Pricing Gap ($15K-$75K/yr)
nodes.rationale: The $15K-$75K/yr price band for self-hosted full-coverage PQC scanners is completely unoccupied globally; open source too engineering-heavy, commercial too expensive

nodes.label: Vendor PQC Readiness Database
nodes.rationale: Built-in knowledge base mapping software versions to PQC support (e.g. OpenSSL 3.4 supports ML-KEM); defensible content moat, nobody has this

nodes.label: Migration Progress Tracking
nodes.rationale: No tool tracks migration progress over time with % complete metric toward 2030/2035; regulatory necessity as deadline approaches

nodes.label: Deployment Simplicity Gap
nodes.rationale: No commercial product deploys in single docker-compose; every tool requires 8-12 weeks professional services; 'first scan within one hour' use case completely unserved

nodes.label: PQC Scanning Market Size
nodes.rationale: Estimated $300M-$500M globally in 2026, growing to $1.5B-$3B by 2028, $5B-$10B by 2031, $15B-$25B by 2035

nodes.label: OSI Layer Cryptographic Inventory
nodes.rationale: Every OSI layer 1-7 has crypto-bearing components; 70-80% of crypto surface at Layer 7 (apps), Layer 4 (TLS), and cross-layer cloud/SaaS

nodes.label: CMDB Adapter Framework
nodes.rationale: Common internal model with vendor-specific adapters behind common interface; each CMDB vendor is 200-800 line plugin; aligns with CSDM/CDM for general assets and CycloneDX CBOM for crypto

nodes.label: Common Internal CI Model
nodes.rationale: Typed schema representing CI, relationship, owner, software, certificate; independent of CMDB vendor; aligned with CycloneDX CBOM and CSDM/CDM

nodes.label: 6-Phase CMDB-from-Scratch Rollout
nodes.rationale: Phase 1 public crypto (days) → Phase 2 CAs (weeks) → Phase 3 internal network (weeks-months) → Phase 4 key stores/HSMs (months) → Phase 5 software/SBOM (months) → Phase 6 code/cloud (months)

nodes.label: Multi-Source Asset Discovery (12 Channels)
nodes.rationale: 12 complementary channels: active probing, passive observation, CMDB, cloud APIs, K8s API, CA/PKI, HSM/KMS, CT logs, code repos, DNS, MDM, endpoint agent; no single channel gives complete coverage

nodes.label: Asset Reconciliation Engine
nodes.rationale: Deduplicates by (ip, port, fqdn) triple; attaches confidence scores to each evidence source; walks relationships; the reconciliation engine is the actual product

nodes.label: Tiered Credential Model (7 Tiers)
nodes.rationale: Tier 0 none → Tier 1 read-only service → Tier 2 local/domain admin → Tier 3 HSM auditor → Tier 4 cloud org read → Tier 5 vendor-specific → Tier 6 repo read-only; no domain admin, no HSM crypto officer

nodes.label: Per-CI Scan Selection
nodes.rationale: CMDB classification tells scanner which scan to run per CI; network device gets SSH audit, database gets TDE query, HSM gets PKCS#11; uniform scan produces 80% noise

nodes.label: PQC Capability Assertion
nodes.rationale: Custom probe that requests hybrid KEX to verify endpoint doesn't just list ML-KEM in config but actually negotiates it under real load

nodes.label: Agentless-First Architecture
nodes.rationale: 80% of PQC discovery is agentless; agents for host-local long tail (TPM, disk encryption keys, endpoint cert stores); hybrid is production default

nodes.label: Structured Finding Schema
nodes.rationale: Rich structured shape: asset, finding_type, algorithm, evidence, pqc_status, quantum_risk_score, hndl_exposure, remediation, cvss_quantum_vector

nodes.label: CMDB-Based Discovery (Approach A)
nodes.rationale: Four-layer integration: connect to CMDB, read and federate CIs, filter for PQC relevance using 4 filter layers, map relationships

nodes.label: PQC Relevance Four Filter Layers
nodes.rationale: Layer 1 class filter, Layer 2 attribute/keyword filter, Layer 3 relationship traversal, Layer 4 priority filter

nodes.label: Feature Comparison (15 Products)
nodes.rationale: Compares 5 open-source + 10 commercial PQC products across 7 functional areas; determines build vs integrate vs skip decisions

nodes.label: PQC Scanner Operational Mechanics
nodes.rationale: Companion to CEO technical strategy; covers day-to-day mechanics of asset discovery, scanning, credentials, agent vs agentless

nodes.label: Web Application Data Flow Deep Dive (v2)
nodes.rationale: Traces browser→CDN→LB→web server→database full data flow; identifies every TLS boundary and crypto at each hop; SAML SSO and OIDC/OAuth2 authentication flows with PQC impact analysis

nodes.label: Protocol Used Column (v2 Enhancement)
nodes.rationale: v2 adds protocol identification to every component table, enabling protocol-specific scanning

nodes.label: PQCrypt Sentinel PQC Discovery Platform
nodes.author: Product Team
nodes.rationale: Enterprise-grade PQC Discovery and Mitigation Platform; automates discovery, identifies quantum-vulnerable algorithms, maps dependencies, scores risk, tracks migration progress

nodes.label: Multi-Dimensional Risk Scoring Engine
nodes.rationale: Factors: HNDL sensitivity, system exposure, business criticality, algorithm vulnerability, regulatory deadline, migration ease, certificate lifetime, data longevity

nodes.label: 9-Stage Migration Workflow
nodes.rationale: Find → Own → Map → Prioritize → Recommend → Pilot → Roll out → Track → Re-scan

nodes.label: Single Docker-Compose Deploy
nodes.rationale: First scan in under 1 hour; on-premise/air-gap capable; no data leaves customer network

nodes.label: MVP Scope (v1.0, 8-12 Weeks)
nodes.rationale: Active TLS scanning, passive TLS/SSH monitoring, PCAP analysis, SSH audit, PQC group probing, hybrid detection, cert chain parse, CT log monitoring, DNS enumeration, CBOM output, risk scoring, vendor readiness DB, web dashboard, docker-compose deploy

nodes.label: Regulatory Framework Alignment
nodes.rationale: NIST FIPS 203/204/205, CISA/NSA, EU DORA, UK NCSC, G7 banking, RBI Cyber Security Framework, SEBI/IRDAI

nodes.label: FastAPI Backend
nodes.rationale: Python 3.12+, async, auto-generates OpenAPI docs, Pydantic validation

nodes.label: Celery Task Queue with Redis Broker
PostgreSQL 16+ Database
nodes.rationale: JSONB for flexible scan output, full-text search, append-only evidence store

nodes.label: React 18+ TypeScript Frontend
nodes.rationale: shadcn/ui + Tailwind CSS; Recharts/ECharts for visualization; React Flow for dependency graphs; Vite build

nodes.label: Modular Monolith Architecture
nodes.rationale: Single deployable with clear internal boundaries; not microservices; small team (2-5 engineers); internal modules can be extracted later

nodes.label: Open-Source Scanner Integration Strategy
nodes.rationale: Wrap existing tools (sslyze, pqcscan, ssh-audit, pyshark, Trivy, Semgrep) as workers; our value is reconciliation, risk scoring, dashboard

nodes.label: Append-Only Evidence Store
nodes.rationale: Scan results immutable; enables scan-over-scan diff, complete audit trail, no write conflicts

nodes.label: Credential Vault Integration
nodes.rationale: MVP: env vars/encrypted config; Phase 2: HashiCorp Vault/CyberArk; Phase 3: customer-managed vault

nodes.label: Security-First Dark Mode Design
nodes.author: Design Team
nodes.rationale: Primary dark mode theme reduces eye strain for security analysts during long sessions; inspired by GitHub, Vercel, Linear, Grafana, Wiz, Snyk

nodes.label: UI Design Philosophy (5 Principles)
nodes.rationale: Clarity over decoration, progressive disclosure, actionable by default, security-first dark mode, data-dense but scannable

nodes.label: Backend Database Schema (11 Tables)
nodes.author: Backend Engineering Team
nodes.rationale: users, connectors, scans, assets, certificates, algorithms, findings, scan_logs, asset_relationships, migration_progress, reports

nodes.label: Assets Table Schema
nodes.rationale: Core entity: name, asset_type (18 types), ip_address, fqdn, port, protocol, environment, business_service, owner_id, discovery_source, cmdb_ci_id

nodes.label: Findings Table Schema
nodes.rationale: 18 finding types, 5 severity levels, pqc_status, risk_score, hndl_exposure, evidence (JSONB), remediation, recommended_algorithm

nodes.label: Migration Progress Table Schema
nodes.rationale: Tracks pqc_readiness (6 stages: not_started through verified), algorithm counts by type, readiness_score over time

nodes.label: NIST FIPS 203/204/205 PQC Standards
nodes.rationale: ML-KEM (FIPS 203), ML-DSA (FIPS 204), SLH-DSA (FIPS 205); RSA/ECDSA disallowed after 2030 (112-bit) and 2035 (all)

nodes.label: RBI Cyber Security Framework
nodes.rationale: Data residency requirements constrain procurement; Indian banks need on-prem-only deployment, INR billing, India-based support

nodes.label: Mosca's Theorem (HNDL Risk Model)
nodes.rationale: If data longevity + migration time > time to quantum computers, then the system is already at risk; per-asset timeline analysis

nodes.label: CSDM/CDM Data Model Alignment
nodes.rationale: ServiceNow CSDM + Microsoft CDM for general asset data; CycloneDX CBOM for crypto-specific data; enables round-trip CMDB→scan→CBOM→CMDB

nodes.label: pyshark Passive TLS/SSH Capture
nodes.rationale: tshark-based passive network monitoring via SPAN port; capability no competitor offers in open-source-friendly way

nodes.label: pqcscan (Rust CLI Tool)
nodes.rationale: ML-KEM/ML-DSA handshake detection; integrated as CLI binary with JSON output

nodes.label: Self-Hosted Mid-Market Positioning ($15K-$50K/yr)
nodes.rationale: The 'self-hosted, full-coverage, sub-hour deploy, $15K-$50K/yr mid-market' zone has zero competitors globally

nodes.label: SAML SSO PQC Impact Analysis
nodes.rationale: SP AuthnRequest signature, IdP Assertion signature, Assertion encryption, SP verification all need PQC; TLS at every HTTPS hop needs PQC KEX

nodes.label: OIDC/OAuth2 PQC Impact Analysis
nodes.rationale: JWT signing by AS needs PQC signing key (ML-DSA-44); TLS at token endpoint and resource server needs PQC KEX; JWKS endpoint must include PQC keys
